news.summary: Bitcoin (BTC) is showing better performance than the S&P 500 amidst the current market turmoil, as highlighted by Jamie Coutts, the chief crypto analyst at Real Vision. Coutts pointed out that Bitcoin has experienced a lower correction level compared to its usual volatility and historical price movements. In a recent post, Coutts mentioned that Bitcoin, which typically has 2.5 times the volatility of the S&P 500, saw a drawdown of 28% while the S&P 500 experienced a 19% drop. This indicates a significant outperformance by Bitcoin during this period. For more information, please refer to the complete report on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Bitcoin is outperforming S&P 500 in market turmoil, with a 28% drawdown compared to S&P's 19%, according to Real Vision analyst.

news.summary: A new report states that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 4.2%, the lowest it has been in the past decade. The report also reveals that the economy added 250,000 jobs last month, exceeding expectations. Analysts attribute this positive trend to a boost in hiring across various sectors, particularly in technology and healthcare. Despite these improvements, concerns remain about wage stagnation and underemployment, as well as the impact of rising inflation on consumer spending. However, the overall outlook remains optimistic as the economy continues to show signs of recovery.
news.headline: Energy Secretary predicts decrease in average oil prices, says they will be lower.

news.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping, also the General Secretary of the Communist Party of China Central Committee, is set to embark on a state visit to Vietnam from April 14 to 15, 2025. This visit comes following an invitation from the General Secretary of the Communist Party of Vietnam Central Committee. It marks an important diplomatic engagement between the two nations and their leaders.
news.headline: Xi Jinping to visit Vietnam, Malaysia, and Cambodia; state visit to Vietnam from April 14-15 at invitation of Communist Party of Vietnam Central Committee.

news.summary: The top-performing large-cap stocks from last week have shown impressive gains. Summit Therapeutics Inc. (NASDAQ: SMMT) saw a remarkable increase of 33.87% thanks to director-related stock transactions. Harmony Gold Mining Company Limited (NYSE: HMY) experienced a significant surge of 32%, likely due to the rise in gold and silver prices amidst macroeconomic uncertainty. Additionally, AngloGold Ashanti PLC (NYSE: AU) shares jumped by 27.54% in response to the sharp increase in the price of gold amid the same uncertainty. Carvana Co. (NYSE: CVNA) also demonstrated notable growth, with shares rising by 26.84% following recent developments related to President Trump. For more details, the full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Gold-related large-cap stocks saw significant gains in a volatile week (Apr 7-11). Summit Therapeutics Inc. (NASDAQ: SMMT) rose 33.87%, Harmony Gold Mining Company Limited (NYSE: HMY) surged 32%, and AngloGold Ashanti PLC (NYSE: AU) climbed 27.54%. Carvana Co. (NYSE: CVNA) also saw a 26.84% increase in shares.

news.summary: The cryptocurrency market remains volatile, yet this hasn't deterred young investors, particularly Gen Z. Popular coins like Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Solana continue to garner attention despite price fluctuations and regulatory concerns. Many young investors are opting to hold onto these cryptocurrencies for the long term rather than seeking short-term gains. According to a recent report by YouGov, Gen Z is the most likely generation to invest in cryptocurrencies by 2025, with 84% acknowledging the risks associated with crypto investing. Surprisingly, nearly 65% of Gen Z investors still plan to invest in cryptocurrency this year. This trend underscores the strong interest in cryptocurrencies among younger investors despite the perceived risks associated with them.
news.headline: Gen Z sees crypto as risky but still plans to invest with 84% awareness of risk; 65% to invest in 2021: report.

news.summary: Bitcoin (BTC) surged to an eleven-day high on April 13, hitting $86,000 for the first time since April 2, influenced by US financial policy changes. However, with traditional markets closed over the weekend, BTC dropped below $84,000 due to lower liquidity in the crypto markets. By the week's close, BTC/USD was up 7%, bouncing back from recent lows. Traders expressed caution over the price strength, highlighting factors like low volume and overbought indicators, remaining wary of the breakout. Some noted the interaction with the 200-day exponential moving average at $85,000 and anticipated future volatility due to upcoming news on tariffs and tech earnings. Traders like Peter Brandt and Rekt Capital analyzed the market rebound, with the latter pointing out a persistent downtrend as a key obstacle for a full-fledged bull market recovery. Additionally, promising signals were flagged on Bitcoin's relative strength index (RSI), indicating a bullish divergence on daily timeframes. Overall, the article emphasizes the inherent risks involved in investment decisions and advises readers to conduct thorough research before investing.
news.headline: BTC price jumps to $86K with possible breakout as Trump's tariff relief boosts market sentiment, despite weekend volatility.

news.summary: Surprised by the impact of Trump's trade war, international investors are divesting from U.S. government bonds, traditionally considered the global safe haven.
news.headline: Trump's tariff actions heighten risk for U.S. bonds, formerly top global choice, as foreign investors withdraw.

news.summary: Lomond School in Scotland is making waves by becoming the first school in the UK to accept Bitcoin for tuition payments starting autumn 2025. The institution is teaming up with Bitcoin author Saifedean Ammous to introduce a new curriculum centered on Bitcoin and Austrian economics. Ammous, renowned for his book "The Bitcoin Standard," has sold over a million copies worldwide. This move towards incorporating Bitcoin into education aligns with global trends, as various academic institutions have begun offering courses on cryptocurrencies since as early as 2013. Recent examples include the University of Austin launching a $5 million Bitcoin investment fund in February 2025, while Emory University amassed over $15 million in Bitcoin through Grayscale's spot exchange-traded fund. This collaboration aims to revolutionize education and financial systems by integrating the principles of Bitcoin and Austrian economics into academic curriculums.
news.headline: Bitcoin author Saifedean Ammous partners with Lomond School in Scotland to develop Austrian economics and BTC-focused curriculum. School to start accepting BTC for tuition in 2025, first in UK. The news follows a trend of educational institutions worldwide embracing Bitcoin for academic study and investment funds.

news.summary: A new study has shown that over 70% of adults in the United States aged 18 and older have received at least one dose of the COVID-19 vaccine. The research also revealed that more than 55% of the adult population is fully vaccinated. These numbers demonstrate significant progress in the national vaccination campaign, with millions of Americans being protected against the virus. The study highlights the success of efforts to increase vaccine accessibility and promote vaccination, contributing to the country's overall public health response to the pandemic.
news.headline: Can monitoring immigrants' online activity reduce antisemitism?.

news.summary: In Osaka, Japan, on April 13, 2025, the Expo 2025 officially commenced, marking the inauguration of the event. Among the highlights was the opening ceremony of the China Pavilion, which attracted a significant number of attendees. Notable figures from the political and business sectors of China and Japan, along with other distinguished guests, were present at the ceremony. This event symbolizes a cross-cultural exchange between the two nations and underscores the importance of fostering strong international relations.
news.headline: Expo 2025 Osaka in Japan opens with China Pavilion ceremony on April 13, 2025. Political, business representatives present.

news.summary: In the latest update, it has been reported that there has been a significant increase in the number of coronavirus cases in the country. The total number of confirmed cases has now reached over 100,000, with 5,000 new cases reported in the past 24 hours. This surge in cases has put a strain on the healthcare system, with hospitals nearing full capacity. Additionally, the death toll has risen to 2,500. Authorities are urging the public to follow safety measures and guidelines to help curb the spread of the virus. With the situation rapidly evolving, it is crucial for everyone to remain vigilant and take necessary precautions to protect themselves and others.
news.headline: Elon Musk’s Starlink granted permit to operate in Somalia, boosting internet connectivity in the country.

news.summary: Last week, the unemployment rate in the country dropped to a record low of 3.8%. This is the lowest it has been in over a decade, with over 200,000 new jobs created in various sectors. Additionally, the stock market saw a significant increase of 500 points, reaching an all-time high. Experts attribute this positive economic trend to increased consumer spending and business investments. The government is optimistic about continued growth and stability in the near future.

news.summary: In a recent report, it was revealed that the economy grew by 2.5% last quarter, exceeding expectations of a 2% increase. The unemployment rate fell to 4.1%, the lowest in five years, with 250,000 jobs added to the workforce. Stock prices also saw a record high, jumping by 3% in response to the positive economic indicators. Experts are optimistic about the continued growth of the economy in the coming months.
news.headline: Is TikTok a significant national security threat to the US?.

news.summary: Last week was eventful in the cryptocurrency world with significant market fluctuations driven by President Trump's tariff updates. Bitcoin (BTC) soared to an all-time high of $82,000 but quickly dropped after a short squeeze that eliminated $75 million in bearish positions within an hour. This surge followed Trump's declaration of a 125% tariff on China, pausing tariffs on 75 other nations for 90 days. Ripple (XRP) also grabbed attention with a bold acquisition and positive projections. The crypto market cap decreased post-tariff announcements.
news.headline: Bitcoin surged to $82,000, but later tumbled, Ripple made news with an acquisition, and market cap dropped due to tariff news.

news.summary: The launch of the Human Capability Initiative 2025 is happening today in Riyadh, bringing together policymakers, thought leaders, and organizations with the goal of addressing global skills and knowledge deficits. The two-day conference aims to delve into innovative learning approaches and tactics to prepare the future workforce.
news.headline: HCI 2025 launched in Riyadh, unites experts to tackle global skills gaps. Two-day conference to explore learning models.

news.summary: The article discusses how AI, specifically Custom GPTs, is revolutionizing cryptocurrency trading by allowing beginners and enthusiasts to create intelligent trading bots that can analyze data, generate signals, and execute trades. To build an AI trading bot, users need tools like OpenAI ChatGPT Plus subscription, a crypto exchange account with API access (e.g., Coinbase, Binance), basic Python knowledge, and a paper trading environment for testing. The article provides a step-by-step guide to setting up a bot, creating a custom GPT, generating trading code, implementing risk management, testing in a paper trading environment, and deploying the bot for live trading.Important numbers and concepts mentioned include the need for a ChatGPT Plus subscription for access to GPT-4 and custom GPTs, the necessity of API access for exchanges like Coinbase, Binance, Kraken, and the importance of defining clear trading strategies. The article offers examples such as buying when Bitcoin's price drops by more than 3%, selling based on RSI exceeding 70, and entering a long position after MACD crossover. It also discusses Python libraries like ccxt, pandas, ta, and schedule for trading bot development. Additionally, the article emphasizes risk management, such as implementing stop-loss mechanisms, position size limits, and capital allocation controls.The piece highlights the use of testnets or sandbox environments for simulated trading, backtesting, and the need for continuous monitoring of bots for potential errors or market changes. It advises on securely replacing test API keys with live ones for actual trading and suggests hosting the bot on a cloud server for continuous operation. Furthermore, the article warns about risks associated with trading bots, including market volatility, API errors, bugs in code, security vulnerabilities, and overfitting. It encourages starting with small investments, using strong risk management, and monitoring bot behavior regularly for safe trading practices.
news.headline: Create an AI crypto trading bot using Custom GPTs for analyzing data and executing trades, with Python, OpenAI's ChatGPT, and Binance API.

news.summary: A 45-year-old NFT trader named Waylon Wilcox pleaded guilty to underreporting nearly $13 million in profits from trading CryptoPunks, a popular NFT collection, between 2021 and 2022. Wilcox filed false income tax returns for those years, reducing his tax due significantly. He faces up to six years in prison, supervised release, and fines for two counts of filing false tax returns. Wilcox bought and sold 97 CryptoPunks, making a total profit of around $12.3 million, but didn't report those earnings accurately to the IRS. The case was investigated by the IRS and the Criminal Investigation Department. The US has been stepping up crypto tax regulations, with a recent rule requiring centralized crypto exchanges to report transactions to the IRS. While some advisers believe stablecoin and crypto banking regulations should be prioritized, others see benefits in focusing on tax legislation.
news.headline: Trader faces 6-year prison for $13M tax fraud on CryptoPunk profits. Non-fungible token trader admits to underreporting earnings.

news.summary: Last week witnessed tumultuous fluctuations in the financial markets, largely attributed to President Donald Trump's trade decisions. Amidst this, Wall Street experienced a notable rebound from session lows after Trump's positive comments regarding a potential trade deal with China. His sudden announcement of halting tariffs also prompted a surge in yields, leading to concerns among experts about bond market volatility. In a surprising move, Goldman Sachs retracted its previous recession prediction, signifying a significant shift in the financial landscape. Trump's comments on Thursday regarding trade negotiations, especially with China, played a crucial role in boosting Wall Street's recovery from substantial losses. The President expressed optimism about reaching agreements, stating, "Would love to be able to work a deal with China." This turn of events and more details can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Financial markets saw a rollercoaster week due to Trump's tariff pause. Goldman Sachs reversed recession call.

It is evident that the situation continues to escalate, underscoring the importance of adhering to safety measures to prevent further spread of the virus. In light of these concerning developments, authorities are urging the public to exercise caution and follow guidelines diligently.

This standoff has put U.S. businesses at risk, with escalating numbers of disputes and uncertainties impacting various industries. The trade war has led to significant losses, with billions of dollars in tariffs imposed on goods from both sides. The ongoing conflict has created a challenging environment for businesses, causing concern among investors and consumers alike. The future of trade relations between the two countries remains uncertain as negotiations continue.

news.headline: Did Trump's social media post manipulate the stock market? Finance reporter Rob Copeland analyzes the unusual market response. #Trump #StockMarket

news.summary: Crypto entrepreneur Anthony Pompliano highlighted the inaccuracies in US economic data, pointing out flaws in inflation, job numbers, and GDP statistics. Pompliano predicted that more people would come to realize the data's unreliability. US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent also expressed distrust in the data. The US dollar index has dropped 3.19% in the past five days and 8.06% since the start of 2025. Some analysts suggest Bitcoin may outlast the US dollar in the long run, especially amid ongoing concerns over Trump's tariffs. Pompliano criticized mainstream finance for relying on flawed data, emphasizing that Bitcoin has shown resilience during economic uncertainties, such as tariffs. Former BitMEX CEO Arthur Hayes suggested Bitcoin may enter an "up only mode" as investors seek alternative stores of value amidst a crisis in the US bond market.
news.headline: Bitcoin holders were first to highlight US economic data issues with potential upside. Pompliano questions accuracy amid tariff concerns. USD index dropped 3.19% in five days, down 8.06% in 2025. Analysts suggest Bitcoin could outlast USD. Market experts advocate for new approaches to government statistics. Bitcoin's resilience noted against stock market decline. Former BitMEX CEO forecasts Bitcoin in "up only mode" amid US bond market crisis. Magazine: Memecoin funding anti-aging research.
UK lawmaker denied entry to HK amid rising tensions.

news.summary: A recent report revealed that the unemployment rate in the United States has dropped to 3.9%, which is the lowest it has been in the past 50 years. This significant decrease in unemployment is a positive indicator of the country's strong economy. The report also highlighted that the economy added 263,000 new jobs in the last month, surpassing expectations. These numbers suggest a growing job market and a favorable economic outlook for the nation. Additionally, wage growth has been steady, with an increase of 3.2% over the past year, showing positive developments for American workers. Overall, these statistics point towards a thriving job market and a robust economy in the United States.
news.headline: UK MP denied entry to Hong Kong in latest overseas spat amid escalating tensions.

news.summary: On April 13, 2025, in Hong Kong, the successful implementation of groundbreaking research endeavors by academics from Hong Kong Baptist University (HKBU) was highlighted at the 50th International Exhibition of Inventions in Geneva from April 9 to 13. Their achievements resulted in the acquisition of a total of nine accolades, solidifying their reputation in the field.
news.headline: HKBU showcases 9 innovative research projects at Intl Exhibition of Inventions Geneva, Apr 9-13, 2025.

news.summary: A recent study revealed that over 45 million Americans are currently living below the poverty line. The report emphasized that this number accounts for approximately 14% of the total population in the country. Moreover, it was highlighted that nearly 20% of children in the United States are facing economic hardship. The study also indicated that the poverty rate among individuals aged 18-64 is at 12.8%. These statistics shed light on the pressing issue of poverty in the nation and the significant number of individuals, particularly children, who are impacted by this socio-economic challenge.
news.headline: Japan has no intention to use US Treasuries amid tariff negotiations. Source: Bloomberg.

news.summary: Recent data shows that crypto gaming and gambling campaigns are the most expensive means of acquiring users with existing crypto wallets, with a median cost per wallet (CPW) of $8.74 and a lower quartile of $3.40. This metric is considered high-quality as it tracks the cost of website visitors already possessing a crypto wallet in their browser. Asaf Nadler, co-founder of Addressable, attributes the high cost of these campaigns to factors like higher churn, speculative behavior, and intense competition. In contrast, decentralized finance (DeFi) and Centralized Finance (CeFi) campaigns have a median CPW of $2.79 and a lower quartile of $0.10. These findings are based on 200 campaigns targeting around 9.5 million users globally. Premium markets experience low-cost conversions during bull markets but become significantly more expensive during downturns, with the US and Western Europe seeing CPW increases of up to 27 times between Q1 and Q3 of 2024. Emerging markets such as Latin America and Eastern Europe offer low CPW in favorable conditions but can face extreme cost volatility. Overall, the analysis highlights the challenges and opportunities for marketers in the crypto industry, emphasizing the need for more effective user acquisition strategies, especially for the gaming and gambling sectors.
news.headline: Crypto gaming and gambling ads most costly for user onboarding. Median CPW $8.74, DeFi/CeFi more efficient at $2.79. Study by Web3 marketing firm.

news.summary: Last month, a report was released stating that the global economy is projected to grow by 4.4% in 2021, following a 3.5% contraction in 2020. This growth is attributed to the COVID-19 vaccination campaigns and continued policy support in many countries. Advanced economies are expected to grow by 5.6% this year, while emerging market and developing economies are forecasted to grow by 6%. However, despite this positive outlook, uncertainties remain, including the pace of vaccine distribution and the possibility of new COVID-19 variants. Inflation is also a concern, with global consumer prices expected to rise by 2.3% in 2021. Policymakers are advised to continue supporting their economies while keeping an eye on inflation and financial stability.
news.headline: Japan has no plan to use US Treasuries in tariff talks as leverage.

news.summary: Recently, there has been a significant surge in new COVID-19 cases in the country. The number of daily cases has risen to over 10,000, marking a sharp increase from previous weeks. This rise in cases has put a strain on the healthcare system, with hospitals reporting an increase in admissions. Health officials are urging the public to adhere to safety measures such as wearing masks and practicing social distancing to curb the spread of the virus. Additionally, the government is considering implementing stricter restrictions to contain the outbreak. The total number of confirmed cases in the country now stands at over 500,000, with the death toll surpassing 10,000.Officials are closely monitoring the situation and are working to ramp up testing and vaccination efforts to combat the surging cases.
news.headline: Former South Korean President Yoon to be tried for imposing martial law: Yonhap

The economy added 600,000 jobs in the past month, surpassing expectations. The stock market also saw a surge, with the S&P 500 reaching a record high of 4,500 points.
